text.title: GraphQL API Design Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the graphql-api-design methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Edit the SDL before resolver code; the SDL diff is what humans review.
rule.rationale: Schema-first aligns frontend/backend on a stable contract; resolvers are derived from the schema, not the other way around.

rule.statement: Every resolver that accesses a related entity uses a DataLoader, instantiated per-request in GraphQL context.
rule.rationale: Module-global DataLoaders cache across requests and leak user data; without DataLoader, N+1 queries pass unit tests but destroy prod performance.

rule.statement: Every list field uses Relay-style cursor pagination (first/after, edges, pageInfo). Offset pagination is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Offset pagination produces duplicates (inserts) and gaps (deletes) under concurrent writes; cursors are stable.

rule.statement: Apply QueryDepthLimiter (max 8-10) and QueryComplexityExtension (max 100-500) on every public schema.
rule.rationale: Without limits, a single deeply nested query can drain the DB connection pool and act as a DoS vector.

rule.statement: Every Query and Mutation field touching auth-scoped data declares permission_classes or an equivalent directive. Auth checks in business logic are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Auth checks scattered in business logic are forgotten on new fields; a per-resolver gate catches missing auth before merge.

rule.statement: Mutations return a typed payload with a union of success type and typed error types; errors[] is reserved for unexpected failures.

rationale.source: GraphQL best practices + Shopify schema design

rule.rationale: Union types make expected failures explicit and force clients to handle them; untyped errors[] signals a bug, not a business rule.

rule.statement: If the API is a cacheable public read API OR single-client CRUD OR file-upload-heavy, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: GraphQL overhead (DataLoader, depth/complexity, persisted queries) is not paid back outside multi-client, nested-read use cases.
